summary: Phase 8 testing completed: Backend 15/15 passed (100%), Frontend 4/5 features passed (80%). Found 1 critical bug in Save Scenario functionality.

integration_issues.issue: Save Scenario button remains disabled after successful scenario simulation. Error in console: 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading warming_c)'
integration_issues.root_cause: The savePayload object is built with result.input?.warming_c, but when the SaveScenarioButton dialog content tries to render scenarioPayload?.warming_c on line 51 of SavedScenarios.jsx, it causes a React error if result.input is undefined or incomplete. This prevents the button from being enabled.
integration_issues.suggested_fix: Add better null checking in Scenarios.jsx savePayload construction. Ensure result.input exists before building savePayload, or add fallback values. Also add error boundary or better validation in SaveScenarioButton component.

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/geo/india/states returns GeoJSON with 35 state polygons
Backend: GET /api/export/historical?state_code=MH&days=90&fmt=csv returns CSV with 90 days
Backend: GET /api/export/drought?fmt=csv returns CSV with 36 states
Backend: GET /api/export/monsoon?fmt=csv returns CSV with 14 states
Backend: POST /api/saved-scenarios (with auth) creates scenario and returns id
Backend: GET /api/saved-scenarios (with auth) returns list of user scenarios
Backend: User isolation - User A cannot access User B's scenarios (returns 404)
Frontend US-19: Drought page renders India choropleth with 35 state polygons filled with SPI colors
Frontend US-20: Dashboard drought layer tab renders polygons on map
Frontend US-21: Export Data dropdowns exist on Dashboard, Monsoon, Drought pages with CSV/JSON options
Frontend US-23: Saved Scenarios dialog opens, shows saved scenarios with Load/Delete buttons, Load functionality works
action_item_for_main_agent: CRITICAL: Fix Save Scenario button in Scenarios.jsx. The button remains disabled after scenario simulation completes due to error 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading warming_c)'. Root cause: savePayload construction assumes result.input exists, but SaveScenarioButton dialog tries to render warming_c which may be undefined. Add proper null checking in savePayload construction (lines 77-92 of Scenarios.jsx) and ensure result.input is fully populated before enabling the Save button. Also add validation in SaveScenarioButton component (line 51 of SavedScenarios.jsx) to handle undefined values gracefully.
